summary: Scorching Brent era set with fiery The Other One and Terrapin Station
blurb: The show delivers a high‑energy second set highlighted by an incendiary The Other One, Terrapin Station and a soaring Foolish Heart. Jerry’s guitar is on fire, Brent’s keyboards drive the band forward, and Phil’s bass shines throughout.
review:
On May 6 1990 at Cal State Dominguez Hills the Dead were in full‑blown Brent‑era mode. The first set closed strong with a heartfelt Foolish Heart and an exuberant Feel Like A Stranger, but it was the second set that cemented this night’s reputation – Terrapin Station surged into a cosmic Space, then erupted into a blistering The Other One that rode on Brent’s psychedelic keys and Jerry’s searing leads. Standing On The Moon followed with a crisp, soaring solo, while Throwing Stones kept the momentum rolling to an uplifting Turn On Your Lovelight encore. Across all available tapes the band sounds tight despite the desert heat; Phil’s bass lines were described as “walking across the room,” Brent’s keyboards were “on fire” and driving the groove, and Bob’s vocal quirks added extra flavor on The Other One. Even where the flat soundboard mix shows its age, the performance shines through – a must‑listen for fans of this hot summer run.

The audience FOH capture (gd90-05-06.schoeps-fob…) offers the clearest window into that energy, but the full‑set SBD from Wiley remains the most complete source. Either way, this concert stands out as one of the high points of the 1990 tour, showcasing a band fully in sync and delivering several classic moments that still resonate today.

rating_calculation.sentiment_rationale: Strong positive sentiment across six of seven recordings (all marked 'positive') with only one mixed review. Reviewers repeatedly praise the high‑energy performance, standout songs (The Other One, Terrapin Station, Foolish Heart) and Brent’s keyboard work, indicating a consistently excellent show.

best_recording.reason: Complete soundboard capture of the full show; despite a flat mix it includes every song and is the most widely referenced source for this concert.

key_highlights: Scorching Brent‑era keyboards drive the band forward
Second set peaks with The Other One and Terrapin Station
Foolish Heart shines as a standout ballad
High‑energy flow kept alive despite extreme heat

band_performance.Jerry: Ripping leads throughout, especially on Standing On The Moon and The Other One; a minor note about cutting off Brent’s rap in Blow Away.
band_performance.Phil: Bass lines praised as walking across the room, providing a solid foundation that shines during the high‑energy jams.
band_performance.Bob: Vocals on The Other One described as crazy with an octave splitter effect adding extra texture.
band_performance.Brent: Keyboards are highlighted as on fire – driving the band, delivering psychedelic effects and some of his best playing of the era.
